message:
Expand workflow orchestration and add watcher benchmarks

Workflow improvements (crates/wa-core/src/workflows.rs):
- Expand workflow state machine
- Add workflow step execution logic
- Improve error handling and recovery

Add crates/wa-core/benches/watcher_loop.rs:
- Benchmark watcher loop performance
- Profile pane discovery and event handling

Update Cargo.toml:
- Add benchmark configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.17","title":"E2E script: pane exclude filter prevents capture (ignored pane absent from search, shown in status)","description":"# Task: E2E script — pane exclude filter

## Goal
Validate that pane selection filters protect privacy and behave predictably:
- excluded panes are visible (as ignored) but not ingested
- excluded panes do not appear in search results

## Scenario
- Spawn two dummy panes:
  1) "observed" pane prints OBSERVED_TOKEN
  2) "ignored" pane prints SECRET_TOKEN and has a title/cwd that matches an exclude rule

- Configure wa with an exclude rule that matches the ignored pane.

Steps
1) Start wa watch with the configured workspace.
2) Wait until the observed token is persisted (via wa robot search).
3) Assert that searching for SECRET_TOKEN returns no results.
4) Assert wa status / wa robot state shows the ignored pane as ignored with a reason.

## Assertions
- Observed pane is captured and searchable.
- Ignored pane is not captured and not searchable.
- Status output makes the ignore decision obvious.

## Artifacts
- Effective config used (including pane rules).
- wa watch logs with decision messages (no secrets).
- wa robot state JSON.
- wa robot search JSON for both tokens.

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- On failure, artifacts show whether the bug is:
  - filter matching
  - ingest mistakenly tailing ignored panes
  - search indexing
  - status/state reporting


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert that SECRET_TOKEN never appears in artifacts/logs (privacy guarantee), not just “not searchable”.
  - Add a control assertion that OBSERVED_TOKEN does appear in search to avoid false negatives.

- Artifact validation:
  - Assert state/status artifacts include the ignore reason so the user can understand why capture was skipped.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.18","title":"E2E script: secret redaction in audit/export (no raw secrets in artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E script — secret redaction in audit/export

## Goal
Prove that end-to-end audit and export paths never leak secrets from action inputs.

This is a trust-critical property:
- users will paste artifacts into issues
- CI will upload artifacts
- we must ensure secrets are not present in these outputs

## Scenario
- Spawn a dummy pane that echoes received input.
- Ensure policy allows a send in a safe context.

Steps
1) Send a string containing a known fake secret pattern (e.g., `sk-FAKESECRET123`) via `wa send` or `wa robot send`.
2) Export audit trail slice for this run (or query audit feed).
3) Assert that:
  - the audit entry exists
  - the raw secret string does NOT appear anywhere in audit export output
  - a redacted placeholder DOES appear (to prove redaction happened, not omission)

## Assertions
- Audit emission records the action.
- Redaction rules remove the secret from:
  - audit DB row(s)
  - audit export output
  - E2E script logs + artifacts

## Artifacts
- Effective config used.
- Audit export JSONL.
- Script stdout/stderr.
- Grep-style proof output (counts of secret matches should be 0).

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The test is deterministic.
- Any failure clearly indicates whether the leak came from:
  - audit emission
  - redaction engine
  - export path
  - e2e script logging


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - The script must scan *all* produced artifacts (logs, JSONL exports, summaries) for the fake secret string.
  - Include at least two different fake secret patterns (OpenAI-style, GitHub token-style) to ensure redactor coverage isn’t overly narrow.

- Failure mode:
  - If a match is found, print the exact artifact filename + surrounding context (bounded) to make the leak diagnosable.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.19","title":"E2E script: workspace isolation (no cross-project DB leakage; --workspace honored)","description":"# Task: E2E script — workspace isolation

## Goal
Prove that wa honors workspace scoping and does not leak data across projects.

This is a critical UX property:
- users run multiple projects in parallel
- agents should not read/write the wrong DB
- debugging depends on knowing which workspace you are looking at

## Scenario
- Spawn a dummy pane that prints a unique token: WORKSPACE_TOKEN.

Steps
1) Start wa watch with --workspace <A>.
2) Wait until WORKSPACE_TOKEN is persisted (wa robot search --workspace <A>).
3) Run wa robot search --workspace <B> for WORKSPACE_TOKEN.

Assertions
- In workspace A: the token is found.
- In workspace B: the token is NOT found.

Optional extension
- Start wa watch in workspace B and ensure it creates a distinct DB.

## Artifacts
- effective config for both workspaces
- wa watch logs
- wa robot search outputs for A and B

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- Failures make it obvious whether the bug is:
  - workspace resolution
  - DB path derivation
  - CLI flag propagation


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert the two workspaces produce distinct DB/log paths (not just search behavior).
  - Add a control assertion that workspace B can find a different token once it has ingested something.

- Artifact validation:
  - Artifacts must include the derived paths (workspace root, db path, log path) to make mis-scoping diagnosable.

-{"id":"wa-4vx.10.2","title":"Criterion benchmarks + budgets (pattern detect, delta extraction, FTS query)","description":"# Task: Benchmarks + budgets

## Goal
Prevent performance regressions on hot paths.

This is the “performance test suite” complement to unit/E2E tests: it ensures wa stays fast enough to run continuously.

## Bench targets (must exist)
- Overlap/delta extraction (hot ingest path)
- Pattern engine quick reject (typical no-match)
- Pattern detection on “typical pane output”
- FTS query latency for common queries on representative DB size
- Watcher loop overhead when idle (per-pane check cost)

## Initial budgets (PLAN §13.4 + Appendix G.7)
These are v0 targets; we can tune later, but they must be explicitly recorded so regressions are real failures, not vibes.

- Quick reject no-match: **< 1µs** for typical non-matching text.
- Pattern detection (typical corpus): **p50 < 1ms**, **p99 < 5ms**.
- FTS query common patterns (DB ~100k captures):
  - **p50 < 10ms**, **p99 < 50ms** (hard cap: < 50ms for common queries).
- Watcher loop overhead (idle): **< 100µs per pane check**.

Notes:
- Budgets should be reported with machine metadata (OS/CPU/rustc/features).
- CI should enforce budgets in a non-flaky way (relative threshold vs baseline, or dedicated perf runners).

## CI integration
- Local-only initially is acceptable if CI is too noisy, but the harness must produce machine-readable artifacts.
- Once stable, integrate into CI (tracked by `wa-nu4.3.4.4`) with:
  - clear pass/fail thresholds
  - artifact upload of benchmark results on failure

## Artifacts & logging
Bench runs must produce debuggable artifacts:
- raw Criterion JSON output
- a short summary (top regressions, top slowest benches)
- build/machine metadata:
  - OS, CPU model, rustc version, enabled features
  - git commit hash when available

## Testing
- Bench harness self-check:
  - Add a small “sanity bench” guaranteed to run so the harness failing to execute is caught early.

- Budget enforcement tests:
  - If budget checks are implemented as code, include a unit test that feeds a fake benchmark JSON and asserts:
    - regression triggers failure
    - outputs include which benchmark regressed and by how much

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bench suite runs locally and produces a baseline.
- Budgets above are documented and emitted in summaries.
- CI enforcement can come later if early CI is too noisy, but the artifacts/logging are in place.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.20","title":"E2E suite completeness: runner --all covers all cases + stable case registry","description":"# Task: E2E suite completeness (case registry)

## Goal
Make sure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` is a comprehensive, maintained suite that:
- runs every **registered** E2E scenario (not just the ones under `wa-4vx.10.*`)
- produces stable artifacts per case
- prints a clear PASS/FAIL summary

This task exists to avoid coupling “runner quality” to “suite completeness”.

## Requirements
- Maintain a single authoritative registry of cases (names + descriptions).
- The registry must also record (at least):
  - prerequisites (e.g., requires WezTerm, requires docker, requires feature flags)
  - whether the case is part of the default `--all` run
  - a human-readable “why this case exists” note (what it protects)

- `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` iterates that registry.
- Each case has:
  - deterministic steps
  - explicit assertions
  - artifacts directory
  - clear failure summary

## Cross-repo case coverage (important)
Some E2E scenarios live outside `wa-4vx.10.*` (for example setup safety, new IPC lanes, and UX overlays):
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10` (wa setup idempotency)
- `wa-4vx.2.7.3` (status_update IPC lane)
- `wa-nu4.1.6.4` (pane reservations)
- `wa-0go.7` (natural-language event descriptions)
- `wa-5em.9` (rollback visualization tests/E2E)
- `wa-dug.7` (environment detection + recommendations)
- `wa-tp4.8` (suggestion engine)
- `wa-nu4.4.3.5` (distributed E2E tests; non-default unless distributed feature enabled)
- `wa-9lh` (quick-fix suggestions E2E)
- `wa-am5` (dry-run mode E2E)
- `wa-jl5` (notification webhook delivery E2E)
- `wa-ugg` (timeline correlation E2E)
- `wa-p3i` (watch-and-notify mode E2E)

Suite completeness means the registry accounts for these cases too.
Implementation options (either is acceptable; pick the simplest):
- unify everything under `./scripts/e2e_test.sh` as runnable `--case` entries, OR
- allow registry entries to delegate to another script (adapter entry) while still producing the same artifacts contract.

## Change discipline
- Adding a new E2E case requires updating the registry.
- CI should catch:
  - “case exists but not in registry”
  - “case is in registry but not included in default suite” (unless explicitly tagged as non-default with justification)

## Registry
- This bead *is* the registry. Update the case list here when new E2E cases are added.

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A developer can run `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` and trust it covers the full default suite.
- The suite remains usable even as cases evolve across phases.
- `wa-nu4.3.3.10`, `wa-4vx.2.7.3`, `wa-nu4.1.6.4`, `wa-0go.7`, `wa-5em.9`, `wa-dug.7`, and `wa-tp4.8` are represented in the case registry (directly or via adapter), with stable artifacts.


## Testing
- Registry correctness tests:
  - Add a check that enumerates available E2E cases and fails if any are missing from the registry.
  - Add a check that fails if the registry references a non-existent case.

- `--all` behavior tests:
  - Include a small dummy registry in test mode to verify:
    - ordering and selection flags
    - summary format
    - per-case artifact dir naming is stable

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.21","title":"E2E script: wa stop shuts down watcher (lock released, restart works)","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa stop

## Goal
Validate the user-facing shutdown path:
- wa watch is running
- wa stop stops it gracefully
- the lock is released
- restart works

This is a key UX reliability story for real users.

## Scenario
- Start mux server and spawn a dummy pane that prints a unique token.
- Start wa watch in workspace A.
- Wait until the token is persisted (wa robot search).
- Run wa stop --workspace A.

Assertions
- wa stop returns success.
- wa watch process exits.
- workspace lock is released.
- Starting wa watch again in workspace A succeeds.

## Artifacts
- wa watch logs
- wa stop logs/output
- effective config

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- Failures clearly indicate whether the bug is in:
  - lock metadata
  - signal handling
  - shutdown flushing


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add a negative case: `wa stop` when no watcher is running must return a clear, non-destructive message (and still exit 0/1 consistently as defined).
  - Assert that lock release is proven by attempting a second start (not by checking files alone).

- Artifact validation:
  - Include watcher PID/lock metadata evidence in artifacts so failures are actionable.

 {"id":"wa-4vx.10.22","title":"E2E script: wa doctor (healthy/broken) with verbose logs + artifacts","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa doctor

## Goal
Prove `wa doctor` works end-to-end as a **deterministic** health-check surface with **excellent logs/artifacts**.

Unit tests can validate individual checks, but this E2E case ensures the full command (arg parsing → config resolution → checks → output) behaves correctly and remains user-friendly.

## Scenario(s)

### Scenario A: Healthy workspace
- Create a fresh temp workspace (isolated DB/log dirs).
- Ensure stubbed dependencies report “healthy”:
  - wezterm CLI available + returns a plausible `wezterm cli list` output
  - DB opens and schema version matches
- Run:
  - `wa doctor --format json`

### Scenario B: Broken workspace (actionable failures)
Run doctor against controlled failures, one at a time:
- Missing wezterm CLI.
- Workspace not writable.
- DB cannot be opened / schema mismatch.

## Assertions
- Exit codes are correct and stable:
  - healthy → `0`
  - broken → non-zero
- JSON output is machine-parseable and contains:
  - a concise overall status
  - per-check status + actionable remediation hints
- No secrets appear in:
  - stdout JSON
  - stderr logs
  - artifact files

## Artifacts / logging contract
- Capture and store per-scenario artifacts in the standard E2E harness layout:
  - stdout (raw)
  - stderr (raw)
  - resolved workspace paths
  - any generated diagnostic files
  - timing summary (start/end + durations per step)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Script is deterministic and can run in CI.
- When it fails, the artifact bundle is sufficient to debug quickly.

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure each “broken workspace” scenario fails for the intended reason (not cascading unrelated failures).
  - Add assertions that the remediation hints include stable `code` values so automation can key off them.

- Artifact validation:
  - Ensure the script stores both the raw doctor output and a normalized “check summary” extraction for quick debugging.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:18:28.342228068Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-19T20:48:30.611278045Z","compaction_level":0,"labels":["area-diagnostics","area-e2e","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:48:30.611218533Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:48:28.402435859Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:18:31.197833225Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:10:26.979033966Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:19:14.058555221Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:29:49.679413396Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"wa-4vx.10.22","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T12:18:47.651043795Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.1.8","title":"Integrate workflow action loop into `wa watch` (auto-handle) while preserving observe/act split","description":"# Task: Integrate workflows into `wa watch`

## Goal
Enable `wa watch --auto-handle` to run workflows in response to detections, while keeping the **observe** loop strictly passive and the **act** loop explicitly gated.

This is the moment `wa` stops being “just a recorder/search index” and becomes a safe automation runtime.

## Core invariants (non-negotiable)
- The observation loop remains passive:
  - no `send-text`, no control sequences, no workflow actions
  - if workflow code is not enabled, wa still captures + indexes reliably
- The action loop is explicit:
  - runs only when enabled by config/flags
  - always re-checks live pane state immediately before each action
  - every action attempt is policy-gated

## Deliverables
- `wa watch` starts a workflow runner/scheduler when configured.
- Config + flags:
  - enable/disable workflow runtime
  - allowlist which workflows may auto-run
  - max concurrent workflows (global + per-pane)
  - `--auto-handle` (opt-in) to act automatically
- Structured logs and audit correlation:
  - a workflow execution id is included on all logs for that run

## Failure and safety behavior
- If policy denies an action during a workflow:
  - workflow step is recorded as denied (not retried blindly)
  - the originating event remains visible to the user/robot as unhandled (or handled_aborted, depending on semantics in `wa-nu4.1.1.10`)
- If workflow engine errors:
  - observation loop continues running
  - action loop stops cleanly and reports an actionable error

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - workflow runner does not start when `--auto-handle` is off
  - action loop does not execute without explicit enable
- Integration tests:
  - given a synthetic detection stream, `wa watch` launches workflow execution and records execution rows
  - denied policy decisions never result in a send attempt
- E2E:
  - `wa-4vx.10.8` compaction workflow end-to-end
  - `wa-4vx.10.9` usage-limit workflow end-to-end
  - `wa-4vx.10.12` restart/resume idempotency

## Acceptance Criteria
- With `--auto-handle` enabled and compaction is detected, the workflow triggers exactly once per event (subject to dedupe/cooldown semantics).
- With `--auto-handle` disabled, detections/events are recorded but no sends occur.
- A denied policy decision prevents any input injection and produces a stable, explainable result (logs + audit/step log).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.1.9","title":"[Robot] `wa robot workflow` (run workflow by name, stable result schema)","description":"# Task: `wa robot workflow`

## Goal
Expose workflow execution to agents as a **stable robot command**.

Command:
- `wa robot workflow <name> <pane_id> [--force]`

## Why
- Agents need a single canonical entrypoint to request automation.
- MCP should delegate to the same underlying engine.

## Behavior
- Validate pane exists.
- Acquire per-pane lock.
- Run workflow via the runner.
- Emit a stable result envelope:
  - workflow name
  - pane_id
  - execution_id
  - status (completed/aborted/failed)
  - summary message
  - optional list of step results (short)

## Safety
- PolicyEngine gates any sends.
- `--force` is still policy-gated; it only bypasses workflow-level “already handled” checks.

## Testing
- Unit tests against synthetic workflows.
- JSON output validated against robot schema (`wa-4vx.7.10`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Works against synthetic workflows in tests.
- If policy denies, the command returns a stable robot error and does not attempt to send.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.2","title":"[EPIC] Workflow: handle_compaction (safe context reinjection)","description":"# Workflow: handle_compaction

## Goal
When an agent compacts its context, automatically re-inject the minimal prompt that restores critical project context.

## Why
Compaction causes the agent to forget important constraints (safety rules, repo conventions, current objectives). A deterministic, immediate prompt keeps agent behavior aligned without relying on “human noticing”.

## Core behavior (PLAN Appendix D.1; made self-contained)
- Trigger: detection event `session.compaction`.
- Each step follows the invariant: **Observe → Act → Verify**.

### Step plan (v0)
1. Acquire per-pane workflow lock.
2. Re-read current pane tail (defensive: guard against stale detections).
3. Guard: ensure the compaction anchor is still present within the last N lines.
4. Guard: abort if pane is `AltScreen` (vim/less/etc.) unless explicitly allowed by config.
5. Guard: abort if we have a `RecentGap`/`OutputGap` (state is uncertain).
6. Stabilize:
   - wait a short stabilization window (default: 2s), OR
   - require a deterministic “compaction complete” marker if/when we have one.
7. Send agent-specific prompt (exact strings; include the trailing newline):
   - Claude Code: `"Reread AGENTS.md so it's still fresh in your mind.\n"`
   - Codex: `"Please re-read AGENTS.md and any key project context files.\n"`
   - Gemini: `"Please re-examine AGENTS.md and project context.\n"`
8. Verify:
   - prefer deterministic markers (OSC 133 prompt boundary) if available
   - otherwise accept a minimal verification (prompt echo / UI marker) and record what was observed
9. Mark event handled and record workflow result + step logs.

### Failure modes (v0)
- Pane disappeared: mark workflow cancelled (do not retry blindly).
- Alt-screen detected: abort safely (unless explicitly allowed).
- Verification timeout: mark failed/paused with a next-step plan (do not spam retries).

## Safety
- Never send into alt-screen.
- All sends are PolicyEngine-gated (PromptActive/AgentWaiting, no gaps, rate limits).
- Rate-limited + deduped via event semantics (no prompt spam).


## Success Criteria
- Compaction events are detected and translated into workflow executions safely (policy-gated, deduped).
- Workflow prompts are agent-specific, idempotent, and leave an audit + step-log trail.
- Guards prevent sending when pane state is uncertain (alt-screen, recent gaps, missing anchor).


## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - Synthetic compaction detection triggers exactly one workflow run and exactly one send.
  - Guards:
    - refuses to act when AltScreen or RecentGap is present
    - refuses to act when compaction anchor is no longer visible
  - Stabilization: does not send before the stabilization condition is met.

- E2E:
  - End-to-end compaction scenario covered with full artifacts via `wa-4vx.10.8`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.2.2","title":"Compaction stabilization strategy (wait stable tail or deterministic marker)","description":"# Task: Compaction stabilization strategy

## Goal
Avoid racing the agent UI while compaction is finishing.

If we send a “refresh”/context reinjection prompt too early, the agent may still be streaming UI updates and miss the instruction.

## Options
- Simple: wait a small fixed window (e.g., 1–2s) after detection.
- Better: wait until tail lines stop changing for N polls.
- Best: use deterministic signal (user-var) if the agent emits one.

## Deliverables
- Implement one strategy now (likely stable tail window).
- Keep the design extensible for future deterministic markers.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests (see `wa-nu4.1.2.4`):
  - stabilization waits the intended condition (stable tail or marker)
  - does not wait forever (hard timeout)
  - does not spam get-text excessively (bounded polling)
- E2E:
  - `wa-4vx.10.8` validates compaction workflow end-to-end with dummy pane + logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- Workflow does not send the refresh prompt until compaction is complete enough that the agent will read it.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.2.3","title":"Configurable post-compaction prompts (per-agent/per-project overrides)","description":"# Task: Configurable post-compaction prompts

## Goal
Allow operators to tune the prompt injected by `handle_compaction` **without code changes**, while keeping safety and determinism.

## Why
Different agents (Codex/Claude/Gemini) and different projects may benefit from slightly different:
- “resume instruction” wording
- context reinjection format
- amount of guidance

But we must ensure prompts remain safe, redacted, and not unbounded.

## Configuration model
- Add a config section for workflow prompt templates, with clear precedence:
  1) per-pane override (optional; keyed by pane id or inferred project)
  2) per-agent override (Codex vs Claude vs Gemini)
  3) global default

- Optional per-project/path overrides (based on pane cwd) are allowed only if the matching rules are deterministic.

## Safety constraints
- Template expansion must be bounded:
  - max prompt length
  - max embedded snippet length
- All embedded context must be redacted.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - config precedence is correct and deterministic
  - template rendering respects length bounds
  - redaction is applied before injection

## Acceptance Criteria
- Changing config changes the prompt content used by `handle_compaction`.
- Bad configs fail with clear error messages (not silent fallback).

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.2.4","title":"Tests for handle_compaction (synthetic detection → one send → step logs)","description":"# Task: handle_compaction tests

## Goal
Ensure compaction handling doesn’t regress.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests (fixture-first):
  - given a synthetic compaction detection and pane state `PromptActive`:
    - workflow runs
    - sends exactly once
    - step logs show completion
  - if pane is `AltScreen`:
    - workflow aborts/denies
  - if compaction anchor is not present in tail:
    - workflow aborts safely (stale detection)

- Logging assertions:
  - failures report which guard/step failed (no “mystery timeouts”)

- E2E linkage:
  - ensure these tests align with the end-to-end scenario in `wa-4vx.10.8` (same prompts/markers).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover both the success path and the primary safety abort paths.
- Failures produce actionable diffs (which guard failed, which step did not match).
